topic: An artist's work is displayed without their permission
raw_output:
[story 1]

The gallery air felt suddenly thick, heavy with the scent of expensive perfume and polished marble. Elias stood near the entrance, his gaze sweeping across the room, cataloging the unfamiliar faces and the unsettling stillness of the crowd. Every muscle in his body was coiled, ready to spring, a low hum of tension vibrating beneath his skin. When he finally spotted the canvas—his work, stark and vibrant—hanging prominently, a cold knot tightened in his stomach. He took a slow, measured breath, forcing his hands to remain loose at his sides, his eyes locked on the intrusion. A sharp intake of breath escaped him, quickly suppressed. "Excuse me," he managed, his voice surprisingly steady, though the tremor in his fingers betrayed the internal struggle.

[story 2]

I moved through the throng like a ghost, my senses hyper-focused on the periphery. The noise of conversation seemed distant, muffled by a strange pressure building behind my eyes. I kept glancing towards the corner where my latest installation hung, a deliberate, almost predatory scrutiny in my posture. My jaw was clenched so tightly I could feel the strain in my molars. When a patron approached, their casual greeting felt like a deliberate probe. "Isn't this piece incredible?" they asked, gesturing vaguely. I met their eyes, my expression carefully neutral, yet every nerve ending screamed a warning. I didn't offer a smile; instead, I simply held their gaze, a silent challenge radiating from me.

[story 3]

The silence in the otherwise bustling exhibition hall was deafening to me. I paced a tight circuit near the centerpiece—my unfinished sculpture—my movements economical and sharp. My hands were tucked deep into my pockets, the fabric of my coat a familiar anchor against the rising tide of unease. I watched the onlookers, their murmurs sounding like static. A sudden shift in the lighting caught my eye, and my focus snapped instantly to the wall where my work was displayed, a visceral jolt running through me. I stopped dead, planting my feet firmly, my breathing shallow and rapid. I felt a distinct need to assess the situation, to find the seam in this unexpected invasion.
